{"key_event":"Polarized digital mobilization and institutional hardball","reasoning":"Federal coercive, surveillance, regulatory, and welfare capacities remained extremely high and expanded in some domains, but legislative obstruction, fiscal strain, and norm erosion slightly reduced effective governing capacity. Civil society mobilized powerfully through Tea Party, Black Lives Matter, LGBTQ, immigrant-rights, and anti-Trump networks, yet polarization, misinformation, inequality, Citizens United, union decline, local-journalism erosion, Shelby County, and Janus weakened broad-based accountability.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":7.9,"state_change":-0.1,"state_power":9.3}
